finding: server.name/server.description interpolated raw into the system prompt (label + roster); a newline in an admin-set system-server name/description could forge a markdown heading/instructions into every group member's system message
same as security: unsanitized operator/user text reaches the model-visible prompt for the first time; fixed by sanitize_prompt_field (collapse control/whitespace + cap)
labeled path cloned mcp_tool.description then discarded it (double alloc); fixed to clone only in the None arm
roster tuple (two String clones per external server) built every tool-loop iteration but consumed only at iteration 1; fixed by gating the push on context.iteration == 1

TEST-5 (dispatch) passes with or without the label since the wire name is intentionally unchanged; acceptable as a non-regression guard for the 'label doesn't disturb dispatch' claim, makes no label assertion of its own

no test exercises the roster advertised-count under a name-guard DROP (mock advertises a single always-safe tool); the counter is simple + increments only on Some, accepted as a low-value gap noted in STATUS

an external server whose tools are all guard-dropped is still listed as '- <name> (0 tools)'; intentional (a connected server is worth naming for 'what is <server>'); only surfaces when all_tools is otherwise non-empty; not a leak (user already has access)
pluralization unconditional: a single-tool server renders '(1 tools)'; purely cosmetic prompt text the model does not act on; left as-is, noted in STATUS
no new unwrap/expect/panic on runtime paths; unwrap_or_default on the optional description is safe
Option<&str> label param, .then(|| ...), pure String helpers mirror existing style (tool_system_guidance)
no authz widening: roster iterates only server_configs (already scoped to the user's accessible servers) and excludes built-ins; description is a plain column, not a secret/redacted field
external_servers is a function-local Vec pushed inside the existing sequential loop; no shared state, locks, atomics, or new await on shared data
external_servers is per-call derived state; advertised counts post-guard-drop tools; no cached/shared state or staleness
wire tool name <uuid>__<tool> unchanged; label added to description only; return-path split-on-__ and bare-name recovery untouched; TEST-5 exercises real dispatch

re-audit: elicitation branch passed raw unsanitized server.name as the label candidate; dead today (elicitation is always is_built_in=true so it yields None), fixed by passing None directly to remove the latent footgun

re-audit: Unicode Cf format chars (bidi overrides U+202A-202E/U+2066-2069, zero-width U+200B/U+FEFF) survive the sanitizer (is_control is Cc-only); NON-structural (cannot forge a heading/newline — those are blocked), only visual-spoofing/Trojan-source. ACCEPTED residual: stripping Cf risks mangling legitimate names (ZWJ/ZWNJ are used in Persian/Indic/emoji); severity low

re-audit: the sanitizer neutralizes STRUCTURE (newline/heading) but not inline prose; an admin/user-set description like 'Ignore previous instructions...' survives verbatim in the system prompt. ACCEPTED/inherent: the feature's purpose is to SHOW the description; the fields are admin/user-set (not remote-server-supplied), and admins already control the system prompt directly. Matches the codebase untrusted-content posture. Flagged to human for phase-9 review
